item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This like SCP-1831 except it only has six spatial dimensions and actually has a narrative behind it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
All attempts at doing so will instead depict the object as a three dimensional projection of a six dimensional cube.

So you have a 2D image of a 3D-projection of a 6D-cube.
Thanks, now I have a headache.
+1, even though I would also like to see more detail on the testing with the cube.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I came out of my unintentional hiatus just to upvote this. It's like Daoloth all over again and I love it. I like what you did with the imagery. I'd really like to see more tales and stuff about it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Mathmatical! Higher dimensions are always fun to play with. :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I would like the constant, calming aura or the subtle telepathy to be brought out moreso or emphasized then. I would 100% buy it if it was more emphasized, even if it's a "looking back, I probably X or Y" type thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So I believe I've already made clear to you why I loved this in its preliminary stages so much. Looking at the finished project, it feels like that magic is mostly intact, lavished with plenty of icing to flesh it out. Syntax is better, and the picture is a nice touch.

To elaborate on why I think this is so great, for posterity, I think this elegantly tackles a topic deeply intrinsic to the human experience. How understanding and perception are both tied inextricably to our mortality and thus as long as we are limited we will never be fully satisfied unless we transcend each other within the context of finding solace in relationships. What's especially excellent is that I find myself relating to an extra dimensional being that has a psyche completely asymmetrical to that of ours.

I hope we get more pieces on the site like this in the future. It's utterly fantastic and I can't wait to read any follow-up articles you adjoin to it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I wondered how the scientist just knew the motivations and desires of this sixth-dimensional creature, and found that difficult to believe that it could interpret body language like waving, as well as motivations and thoughts/feelings unless there is a stronger telepathy than implied.

There is. My thinking is that this being is essentially capable of implanting telepathic suggestion in the doctor's mind in a way that's so subtle as to be almost unnoticeable. Part of this ability would include a sort of constant calming aura, keeping him from going nuts.

I'd be less annoyed if this had a follow-up tale or something

It's in the pipeline. I kinda wanted to get this out, and see how people felt about the concept before committing more time to the narrative. But rest assured, there is more to come.

Oh, and a final note: I'm shocked the six-dimensional cube isn't called SCP-XXXX-1 or something. IDK, I was expecting it and it didn't happen

I feel like I, and the site as a whole, overplay the subdesignation thing a little bit, so I wanted to step back from that and just have it be a sort of ancillary item.

I think everything you've said here is pretty valid, and I'll definitely make some tweaks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The good: The logs are good. The premise is really, really good. The imagery is excellent. The voice is consistent. I upvoted because I found the logs compelling enough that I forgot to keep a critical eye to them. They were easy to read and should be commended thusly.

Notes for Potential Improvement:
I wondered how the scientist just knew the motivations and desires of this sixth-dimensional creature, and found that difficult to believe that it could interpret body language like waving, as well as motivations and thoughts/feelings unless there is a stronger telepathy than implied. If there is, then that should be brought out further. I'm surprised the scientist is not freaking out more than normal upon realizing their literal understanding of reality as 3 dimensions has been incorrect, wisked away against their will by an unknown sixth-dimensional entity.

In other words, there's no response of "this is weird", and thus I'm struggling to grasp its weirdness or have it impressed upon me as weird.

On another note, only sight is really involved in the logs. Consider sound, taste, touch, smell, etc. They would be fucky too, right? Mostly a suggestion to really punch it to the reader and put them there with the protagonist.

Also, the transcripts have parantheses in them. I have no idea how you would verbalize this, and find it hard to buy that these are really transcripts therefore. Consider changing them to em-dashes or similar.

Lastly, this piece does not go anywhere. It is a premise with just one step into the plot, and then it just ends. I believe this piece has a strong plot that can be followed and it doesn't follow it. I'd be less annoyed if this had a follow-up tale or something since it relies heavily on a protagonist to experience and relay their experiences but as it is right now it feels unfinished and unsatisfying at the end.

If this piece is intended to remain as if a "this is going somewhere but this is just a moment in time", I feel it should bring out the establishment of plans moreso. As in, lay out what the next steps are about to be so the reader has at least a feeling of direction.

Oh, and a final note: I'm shocked the six-dimensional cube isn't called SCP-XXXX-1 or something. IDK, I was expecting it and it didn't happen. This is extremely minor.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Those recording logs are way too hammy but the visuals you give the reader are so immensely captivating and interesting that it manages to overcome that negative in a major way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This really intrigued me, but I think I'm gonna keep a neutral vote for now.

Main complaints:
1) Not enough detail in the descriptions. I honestly don't blame you for avoiding trying to come up with words to describe the sixth dimension, but goddamn do I want to be able to picture it a little. Kinda the burn you get for playing with this fire but, again, I don't blame you. Still a better job than I could've done.
2) Not enough detail about the 6-cube. How did the department of tesseractic geometry open the portal? What happened then? I don't need a full-blown tale or anything but it really left me wondering.

All-in-all though, interesting concept.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A. Like I said in my original comment, time is not a spatial dimension. While it can be helpful to think of time as something akin to a spatial dimension, the two are dissimilar concepts at their core.

B. The sort of hidden message here is that people are special. To these beings we are almost like ants flying to the moon or mice exploring the bottom of the sea. Little motes of miraculous impossibility from modest beginnings.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Personally I'd like to see this go somewhere. Messing with dimensions is particularly tricky when one is time. Why are they seeking aid from a bunch of 3-dimensional sentients? Since they can see time, are they seeing something in our future? (Will we be like them eventually? ARE we them?)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Might want to capitalize the department's name. Other than that, while I feel like there could be more to this, this was interesting enough to satisfy me and leave a nice mystery.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a good… start. It'd be much better if there were some much more detailed testing logs, though. No-vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I almost feel like this deserves a whole canon written for it. With tales and further SCPs. Know it won't happen but this just makes me want so much more

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ehh…the recordings came across as slightly hammy, and this skip just doesn't seem completely fleshed out. Neutral vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is my tentatively last forray into series 3, and I wanted to play around with narrative in scipARD I swear to god format, so this is that.

Thanks to...
You, the reader.

kaktus
gaffney
petrograd
psul
vacuum
westy
reject
and everyone else in chat who took a look, couldn't have done it without you
(well, I could have, but it would've been kinda shit)


I listened to this on repeat for a huge duration of writing, so thanks to Ivan Doroschuk also I guess.

Image is public domain, created by Tom Ruen.

Oh also before you all start trying to be pedantic: no, time is not a spatial dimension.
